Jharkhand.source: Central Electricity Authority (CEA), CO2 Baseline Database for the Indian Power Sector, Version 19.0 (2023-24)
Jharkhand.notes: Coal-belt state with heavy thermal dependence; grid intensity exceeds the Eastern regional average (0.96). Adjusted upward to reflect Jharkhand's outsized contribution to the eastern thermal fleet.

Kerala.source: Central Electricity Authority (CEA), CO2 Baseline Database for the Indian Power Sector, Version 19.0 (2023-24)
Kerala.notes: Hydro and renewable energy mix significantly lowers grid carbon intensity compared to the Southern regional average (0.74). Value adjusted downward; actual intensity varies year-to-year with monsoon hydrology.

Madhya Pradesh.source: Central Electricity Authority (CEA), CO2 Baseline Database for the Indian Power Sector, Version 19.0 (2023-24)
Madhya Pradesh.notes: Coal-dominant generation mix; adjusted approximately +10% above Western regional average (0.79) to reflect Madhya Pradesh's large thermal installed base.
